title: Scilla depressa
taxonomy: Scilla depressa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is a member of the genus Scilla, which comprises various species of spring-flowering bulbs. Scilla depressa is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. The plant is characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and narrow, linear leaves. Further classification of Scilla depressa at the infraspecific level is not well-documented.

intro: Scilla depressa, also known as Squill or Autumn Squill, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and bulbous rootstock. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, making it a potential treatment for various respiratory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Scilla depressa include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Scilla depressa is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from coughs and colds to skin conditions and wounds.
